paper_claim: CESE-OCN differs from RIGOURATE in output granularity (four-class relation label vs. continuous scalar) and target (high-risk action-overclaim sub-class vs. broad overstatement severity)
evidence_source: §II.A Table X + §VI.E proxy baseline (proxy NOT official RIGOURATE)
allowed_wording: CESE-OCN outputs a four-class relation label and isolates strong_action_overclaim as a separate high-risk class; RIGOURATE outputs a continuous overstatement score. The two are complementary.
forbidden_wording: CESE-OCN outperforms RIGOURATE; Official RIGOURATE reproduced; R4 beats the official RIGOURATE system
revision_needed: No — already correctly worded in V3.14

paper_claim: SimClaim is a controlled counterfactual diagnostic set, not a natural-prevalence corpus
allowed_wording: SimClaim is a controlled counterfactual diagnostic set: evidence spans are drawn from real scientific papers while claim variants are generated to instantiate specific evidence-sufficiency relations. The balanced 1:1:1:1 design is a deliberate construction for pressure-testing boundary recognition under matched evidence.
forbidden_wording: SimClaim is a natural-prevalence corpus; SimClaim estimates the real-world prevalence of strong_action_overclaim; SimClaim claims are natural author-written claims
revision_needed: No — already correctly worded in V3.14

paper_claim: R4 complements LLM judges (screening-oriented), does not replace them
evidence_source: §VI.B matched 100 (R4 macro-F1=0.3280 < LLM 0.5523; R4 strong_F1=0.3000 > LLM 0.0769)
allowed_wording: R4 is a screening-oriented complement to LLM judges, not a replacement and not a state-of-the-art overstatement detector. R4 trades overall macro-F1 for targeted strong_action positive-F1.
forbidden_wording: R4 outperforms LLM overall; R4 beats LLM; R4 is the best model; R4 is stronger than LLMs on all metrics
revision_needed: No — already correctly worded in V3.14

paper_claim: R4 has lower macro-F1 than LLM judges (a known trade-off, not a defect)
evidence_source: §VI.B matched 100 (R4 macro-F1=0.3280 vs. GPT 0.5523 vs. DeepSeek 0.5248)
allowed_wording: R4's macro-F1 (0.3280) is substantially lower than GPT-5.5 (0.5523) and DeepSeek-V3 (0.5248) on the same 100 matched samples. R4's value is relation-specific: it targets the strong_action_overclaim relation that LLM judges miss under the tested prompts.
forbidden_wording: R4 outperforms LLM overall; R4 beats LLM; R4 is stronger than LLMs on all metrics; R4 macro-F1 is comparable to LLM
revision_needed: No — already correctly worded in V3.14; this fact is preserved in V3.14

paper_claim: R4 improves strong_action screening (strong-F1 0.2408 → 0.3967, +0.1559, 10/10 seeds positive)
allowed_wording: On the controlled silver-stage evaluation, R4 improves strong-action positive-F1 from 0.2408 to 0.3967 (+0.1559, CI [0.1058, 0.1988], 10/10 seeds positive) while preserving contradiction and escalation performance. R4 provides targeted recall-improving screening signal for strong_action_overclaim at an operationally acceptable FP/TP ratio.
forbidden_wording: R4 solves strong_action_overclaim; R4 fully handles strong action overclaims; R4 is validated for deployment
revision_needed: No — already correctly worded in V3.14

paper_claim: Scalar overstatement score cannot fully replace relation-specific screening (proxy baseline finding)
evidence_source: §VI.E RIGOURATE-style proxy baseline (proxy NOT official RIGOURATE; nli_scalar_score BLOCKED)
allowed_wording: On the controlled silver-stage evaluation, a scalar score (whether cue-based or LLM-derived) cannot reliably distinguish mild_scope_overclaim from strong_action_overclaim (ROC-AUC ≤ 0.59) and cannot match R4's strong_action screening F1. Scalar overstatement scoring and relation-specific screening are complementary.
forbidden_wording: The scalar baseline is the official RIGOURATE system; The blocked nli_scalar_score baseline was completed; R4 outperforms the official RIGOURATE system
revision_needed: No — already correctly worded in V3.14; proxy explicitly labelled

paper_claim: Balanced 1:1:1:1 class design is diagnostic, not natural prevalence
allowed_wording: The four-way balanced design (111 per class) is a deliberate construction for pressure-testing boundary recognition under matched evidence, not an estimate of the natural prevalence of these relations in real scientific writing. In natural scientific corpora, we expect contradiction_candidate and strong_action_overclaim to be substantially rarer than supported and mild_scope_overclaim.
forbidden_wording: SimClaim estimates the real-world prevalence; The silver-stage screening finding transfers to natural scientific writing; SimClaim is a natural-prevalence corpus
